name: Joint Base Charleston
profile: Military-friendly Joint Base Charleston housing snapshot anchored to North Charleston, Goose Creek, Summerville, Hanahan, and the broader Charleston metro, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Stable coastal military metro with affordability pressure, strong employment, and neighborhood-specific buyer leverage

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 South Carolina VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: South Carolina property taxes can be favorable compared with many military markets, but coastal insurance and flood-zone exposure can materially affect true monthly cost.
Charleston-area buyers should compare mortgage, taxes, insurance, HOA, utilities, flood insurance, commute, and traffic together.
North Charleston, Goose Creek, Hanahan, and Summerville often provide more practical military-family affordability than downtown Charleston or beach-adjacent areas.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan planning estimate for practical Joint Base Charleston-area housing, excluding high-cost downtown and island markets.

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated joint military installation with controlled access.
crime_status.metro_charleston: Neighborhood safety varies across North Charleston, Goose Creek, Summerville, Hanahan, Ladson, and Charleston proper. Military families should evaluate safety, commute, schools, flood exposure, and property condition at the address level.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 school system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: School quality varies significantly across Charleston, Berkeley, and Dorchester County school zones. Summerville, Hanahan, Goose Creek, and selected suburban zones are commonly evaluated by military families.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by military demand, port/logistics employment, Boeing, healthcare, tourism, and strong Charleston metro population growth.

rental_metrics.notes: Planning estimate based on 3-bedroom single-family rental pressure across North Charleston, Goose Creek, Hanahan, and Summerville.

special_events: College of Charleston and regional sports events

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Major joint-base environment with Air Force, Navy, port, logistics, aviation, medical, tourism, and defense-contractor infrastructure across the Charleston metro.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Joint Base Charleston offers stronger affordability in North Charleston, Goose Creek, Hanahan, and Summerville than in downtown Charleston, Mount Pleasant, or island-adjacent luxury markets.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers who compare commute, flood risk, insurance, school zones, and all-in cost in practical military-family corridors.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Joint Base Charleston gives military families access to a strong coastal economy, but the best housing choices are practical suburbs that balance BAH, commute, flood exposure, schools, and long-term resale.

summary_points: Joint Base Charleston sits inside a strong coastal metro with durable military, logistics, aviation, port, healthcare, and tourism employment support.
North Charleston, Goose Creek, Hanahan, Ladson, and Summerville are more practical for military buyers than downtown Charleston or beach-adjacent markets.
Flood risk, insurance, HOA, traffic, and school-zone variation can materially change the true housing decision.
The market is not cheap, but it can be workable for disciplined VA buyers who avoid overpaying for lifestyle premiums.
Renting may be smarter for shorter assignments or families not ready to absorb coastal ownership risks.
opportunities: Strong long-term housing demand from military, port, logistics, Boeing, healthcare, tourism, and defense-related employment.
Practical suburban corridors can offer better BAH fit than downtown or coastal lifestyle markets.
VA buyers may find strong resale relevance when homes balance commute, schools, flood risk, and condition.
Improving inventory can create more negotiating room than peak-market conditions.
Charleston’s broader economic stability can support long-term owner and investor confidence.
risks: Flood-zone exposure and coastal insurance can materially change all-in ownership cost.
Traffic can reduce quality of life even when a home appears close on a map.
Downtown Charleston, Mount Pleasant, Daniel Island, and beach-adjacent areas can stretch military budgets quickly.
School quality and neighborhood fit vary sharply by exact address.
Short assignments may not justify transaction costs if the user buys at the wrong price or in a weaker resale pocket.
buyer_notes: Joint Base Charleston buyers should not choose based only on distance to base; commute route, gate, flooding, insurance, schools, and resale matter together.
North Charleston can be practical, but neighborhood-level screening is essential.
Goose Creek, Hanahan, Summerville, and Ladson often deserve strong comparison for family fit and payment discipline.
Flood insurance, HOA, property condition, and traffic should be included before calling a home affordable.
This market rewards buyers who avoid coastal lifestyle creep and focus on durable military-family value.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around commute logic, flood-risk clarity, school zones, condition, and military-family practicality.
Move-in-ready homes with strong route access and clear insurance/flood information can stand out.
Military buyers respond well to practical monthly value, VA readiness, and low-friction PCS timing.
Homes in strong suburban corridors can benefit from broader Charleston demand if priced realistically.
investor_angles: Military, port, logistics, healthcare, tourism, and defense-related demand support long-term rental relevance.
Investors should stress-test flood risk, insurance, HOA, maintenance, vacancy, and property management.
Cash flow may be tighter than lower-cost military markets because acquisition prices are higher.
Clean single-family homes in practical commute corridors may be stronger than expensive lifestyle-market rentals.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly cost as the primary decision filter.
Compare North Charleston, Goose Creek, Hanahan, Summerville, and Ladson by commute, school zone, flood risk, insurance, and resale appeal.
Verify flood zone and insurance estimates before finalizing a purchase budget.
Avoid stretching into downtown, island, or lifestyle-premium areas unless income and assignment length justify it.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, commute risk, and assignment-length exposure.
seller_guidance: Price against current buyer payment sensitivity and rising insurance awareness.
Highlight commute logic, VA readiness, flood-zone clarity, school-zone appeal, and low-maintenance condition.
Homes with updated roof, HVAC, drainage, windows, and strong inspection confidence should be positioned clearly.
Military buyers need practical value and confidence in monthly cost, not just Charleston lifestyle language.
landlord_notes: Military and civilian employment demand can support stable rental occupancy.
Single-family rentals in strong commute corridors may appeal to military families avoiding purchase risk.
Investors should model insurance, HOA, flood exposure, maintenance, and turnover conservatively.
Practical homes near base, services, and schools may outperform expensive lifestyle rentals for PCS-focused tenants.

market.market_type_summary: Stable coastal military market with affordability pressure and neighborhood-specific opportunity

base_profile.market_label: Charleston / Joint Base Charleston Military Housing Market
base_profile.installation_type: Joint Base / Mobility / Naval Weapons Station / Logistics / Airlift Support
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 628th Air Base Wing / Air Mobility Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Joint Base Charleston is a major joint installation supporting air mobility, logistics, naval weapons station operations, port and transportation missions, joint-base support, and military operations across the Charleston region.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Coastal, high-demand, joint-service, commute-sensitive, flood-aware, family-relevant, and strongly tied to neighborhood, school-zone, insurance, and traffic decisions.
base_profile.base_bluf: Charleston is not just a beautiful coastal PCS. It is a BAH, traffic, flood-risk, insurance, school-zone, and resale decision. Military families should compare practical suburbs by all-in monthly cost and route reliability before chasing lifestyle neighborhoods.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize flood-zone verification, insurance quotes, school-zone confidence, commute testing, BAH fit, and neighborhood safety before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, rent control, roommate flexibility, parking, and access to daily services without overpaying for Charleston lifestyle premiums.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Charleston housing conversations are about commute realism, flood/insurance clarity, BAH pressure, school-zone confidence, and coastal affordability — not generic Charleston lifestyle appeal.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Joint Base Charleston is influenced by military turnover, port/logistics employment, Boeing, healthcare, tourism, and the shortage of clean, practical single-family housing in good commute corridors.

base_map_image.alt: PCSUnited public-facing Joint Base Charleston placeholder orientation image for base housing and relocation planning.
base_map_image.title: Joint Base Charleston Base & Housing Overview
base_map_image.caption: Placeholder PCSUnited orientation image until a dedicated Joint Base Charleston gate and neighborhood map is available.
base_map_image.safety_note: This is not an official DoD map. It is a placeholder PCSUnited orientation image and should not be used for access control, emergency routing, or official navigation.

visitor_control_center.name: Joint Base Charleston Visitor Control Center
visitor_control_center.address: Dorchester Road / Air Base visitor processing area, Joint Base Charleston, SC
visitor_control_center.phone: Verify current phone number with official Joint Base Charleston sources
visitor_control_center.hours: Commonly listed as Monday-Friday, 6:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.; closed weekends and federal holidays; verify current hours before travel
best_for: Family members without routine installation access
visitor_control_center.map_zone: Air Base / Dorchester Gate visitor processing area
visitor_control_center.note: Use official Joint Base Charleston visitor information before arrival. Access requirements, sponsor rules, gate procedures, and hours can change.

gates.name: Dorchester Gate
gates.hours: Often listed as 0500-2100 or updated access depending on current policy; verify official gate status before travel
gates.map_zone: Air Base / Dorchester Road main access area
gates.commute_notes: Primary Air Base access point for many users approaching from North Charleston, Summerville, Ladson, or Dorchester Road corridors.
gates.user_warning: Verify current gate status with official Joint Base Charleston sources before travel.

gates.name: Rivers Gate
gates.hours: Commonly listed as 24/7; verify official gate status before travel
gates.commute_notes: Useful for users living near Hanahan, Goose Creek, or I-26 corridors depending on final duty location.
gates.user_warning: Gate access can change due to construction, security posture, or local policy.

gates.name: Hunley Gate
gates.hours: Commonly listed as 24/7; verify official gate status before travel
gates.map_zone: west / Hanahan and I-26 access area
gates.commute_notes: Important alternate access point for commuters approaching from Hanahan, Goose Creek, and parts of North Charleston.
gates.user_warning: Verify current gate status and route suitability before relying on this gate.

gates.name: Commissary Gate
gates.hours: Often listed as limited / outbound-only during weekday periods; verify official gate status before travel
gates.commute_notes: Not a universal default gate. Use only when current access rules and direction of travel support it.
gates.user_warning: Limited or outbound-only gate rules matter. Do not use this as a default route without checking official hours.

gates.name: Naval Weapons Station Gates
gates.hours: Verify official Naval Weapons Station gate hours before travel
gates.map_zone: Goose Creek / Red Bank Road area
best_for: Users assigned to Naval Weapons Station side
gates.commute_notes: Users assigned to the Weapons Station side should plan housing and commute around Goose Creek, Red Bank Road, and station-specific access routes.
gates.user_warning: Air Base and Weapons Station commute logic can be different. Confirm duty location before selecting housing.

major_services.name: Joint Base Charleston Medical Clinic
major_services.address: Verify current clinic address before travel
major_services.phone: Verify through official TRICARE / Joint Base Charleston medical source
major_services.hours: Verify current clinic and pharmacy hours
major_services.user_note: Treat this as a major medical anchor for PCS planning. Verify emergency care options, pharmacy procedures, and clinic availability directly with official medical sources.

major_services.name: Joint Base Charleston Commissary
major_services.address: Verify current commissary address before travel
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with DeCA
major_services.user_note: Commissary access can materially affect monthly food budget for eligible users in a higher-cost coastal metro.

major_services.name: Joint Base Charleston Exchange
major_services.address: Verify current Exchange location before travel
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with AAFES
major_services.user_note: Important daily-life anchor for new arrivals, families, and permanent party.

major_services.name: Military Clothing Store
major_services.user_note: Useful for military-specific shopping needs after arrival.

major_services.name: Joint Base Charleston Air Base Housing Office
major_services.address: Verify current Air Base housing office address before travel
major_services.email: Verify current housing office email through official sources
major_services.user_note: Military families should contact the correct housing office before committing to on-base or off-base housing.

major_services.name: Joint Base Charleston Weapons Station Housing Office
major_services.address: 2316 Red Bank Road, Ste 100, Goose Creek, SC 29445-8601
major_services.hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.; verify current hours
best_for: Goose Creek / Red Bank Road housing planning
major_services.map_zone: Weapons Station / Goose Creek housing support area
major_services.user_note: Weapons Station housing and Air Base housing planning may involve different commute logic. Users should confirm duty location before choosing a neighborhood.

major_services.name: Unaccompanied Housing Office
major_services.address: Verify current office location before travel
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with official installation sources
major_services.email: Verify current email with official installation sources
major_services.user_note: Eligibility and availability should be verified directly with the housing office.

major_services.name: Bank / Credit Union / ATM Services
major_services.address: Verify current branch or ATM location before travel
major_services.user_note: Specific bank or credit union locations may change. Use current official branch locator or base directory before routing users.

major_services.name: Legal / Finance / Admin Services
major_services.address: Verify current office location before travel
major_services.user_note: Use this as a planning category in the Base tab; exact office routing should point to official base directory links.

major_services.name: Child / Youth / Family Support Services
major_services.address: Verify current CDC, youth center, and school liaison locations before travel
major_services.user_note: Critical for families. Exact program availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified with official Joint Base Charleston resources.

on_base_housing.housing_type: Privatized and military housing support environment across Air Base and Weapons Station communities
on_base_housing.housing_office_address: Air Base and Weapons Station housing offices; verify correct office based on assignment side
on_base_housing.housing_office_email: 628ces.ceac.wshousing@us.af.mil for Weapons Station; verify Air Base contact through official sources
on_base_housing.map_zone: Air Base and Weapons Station housing support areas
best_for: Families wanting to reduce commute uncertainty
Users assigned to either the Air Base or Weapons Station side
watchouts: Availability and waitlists can change
On-base housing may capture BAH differently than off-base renting
Pet policies and breed restrictions should be verified
School assignment must be verified by exact address
Flood risk, insurance, and commute tradeoffs should still be compared against off-base options
Air Base and Weapons Station commute logic can be meaningfully different
on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. Around Joint Base Charleston, the best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, duty location, debt, commute tolerance, school priorities, flood/insurance exposure, pets, and expected assignment length.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Goose Creek
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Strong military-family option with practical access to the Weapons Station side, suburban housing, and generally better affordability than high-cost Charleston lifestyle markets.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-35 minutes depending on exact address, duty location, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Rivers Gate or Hunley Gate depending on Air Base duty location
Other gates depending on current access rules
watchouts: Commute differs heavily depending on Air Base versus Weapons Station assignment
School zones should be verified by exact address
Flood and insurance assumptions should be checked
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong first-look area for families who want practical pricing, military relevance, and access to the Weapons Station side.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Hanahan
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Close-in practical option with strong access to Air Base routes and family-friendly appeal in selected pockets.
best_for: Users wanting proximity without downtown pricing
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-25 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
watchouts: Inventory can be limited
Prices may be higher than farther-out corridors
Exact flood zone and school assignment should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Excellent commute-oriented option if the payment fits and the property is in a strong pocket.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Summerville
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Popular suburban family corridor with more inventory, newer homes, and lifestyle appeal, but commute can stretch during peak traffic.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-50+ minutes depending on exact address, gate, I-26 traffic, and report time
likely_gate_logic: Hunley Gate depending on route and duty location
watchouts: I-26 and suburban growth traffic can be frustrating
Commute must be tested during actual report-time windows
Some newer communities include HOA costs and higher insurance assumptions
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong family and newer-home option if the household accepts commute risk and keeps all-in cost controlled.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Ladson
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Practical middle-ground corridor between Summerville, North Charleston, and Goose Creek with workable affordability.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-40 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
watchouts: Neighborhood quality varies
School zones should be verified
Traffic and route choice can matter more than distance
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good value candidate for users who want practical access without paying premium Charleston prices.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: North Charleston Practical Pockets
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Closest broad market to the Air Base with mixed affordability and convenience, but requires strong address-level screening.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-25 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and traffic
watchouts: Neighborhood safety varies significantly
Property condition and resale appeal should be reviewed carefully
Flood and drainage issues should be verified
School zones vary by address
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Use North Charleston selectively. It can be practical and close, but the best choices require strong property and neighborhood due diligence.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Moncks Corner
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: More space and suburban growth potential north of the core metro, but commute length and traffic must be tested carefully.
best_for: Families wanting more home for the money
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 35-60+ minutes depending on exact address, duty location, gate, and traffic
likely_gate_logic: Rivers Gate or Hunley Gate depending on Air Base duty location
watchouts: Commute may become draining
Route reliability should be tested
Newer subdivisions may include HOA and infrastructure tradeoffs
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Best for families prioritizing space and affordability who can tolerate the commute.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: West Ashley Selected Pockets
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: More Charleston lifestyle access with moderate commute potential, but prices and traffic can stretch some BAH profiles.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 25-50+ minutes depending on traffic, bridge routes, and gate
likely_gate_logic: Rivers Gate or Hunley Gate depending on route
watchouts: Traffic can materially affect daily life
Flood zones should be checked carefully
Prices can stretch military budgets
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Only makes sense when lifestyle value, income, and commute tolerance justify the higher cost.

commute_intelligence.overall_commute_posture: Moderate to challenging depending on duty location, gate, I-26 exposure, and whether the user is assigned to the Air Base or Weapons Station side.
primary_commute_risks: Confusing Air Base versus Weapons Station commute logic
I-26 congestion from Summerville, Ladson, and Moncks Corner corridors
Choosing by mileage instead of gate route
Flooding, heavy rain, bridge traffic, and coastal weather disruptions
planning_guidance: Confirm whether the user is assigned to the Air Base or Weapons Station side before comparing housing.
Test the commute during the user’s expected report-time window.
Compare Goose Creek, Hanahan, North Charleston, Summerville, Ladson, and Moncks Corner by route reliability, not just distance.
Check flood zones and insurance quotes before treating a property as affordable.
Use commute plus all-in housing cost as the core PCSUnited decision screen.
commute_intelligence.pcsu_bluf: Charleston commute decisions are strategic. A house that looks close can still be wrong if the gate route, traffic, flood risk, or duty-location side does not fit.

bah_market_reality.overall_position: Favorable with discipline, but not universally cheap.
bah_market_reality.buying_power_summary: BAH can support buying in practical corridors like Goose Creek, Hanahan, Ladson, North Charleston, and parts of Summerville, but downtown and coastal lifestyle areas can quickly exceed safe monthly limits.
bah_market_reality.renting_summary: Renting can be smart for short assignments, uncertain duty location, or users not ready for flood/insurance ownership exposure.
watch_items: Air Base versus Weapons Station assignment
bah_market_reality.pcsu_strategy_note: Joint Base Charleston should be modeled as a strong but selective market. PCSUnited should test rank, BAH, debt, savings, credit score, family size, duty location, commute tolerance, flood exposure, and assignment length before recommending buy versus rent.

strengths: Strong joint-base and military support ecosystem
Major coastal metro with broad employment and amenities
Access to beaches, downtown Charleston, healthcare, schools, and recreation
watchouts: Traffic and commute friction can be significant
Flood risk and insurance must be verified
School zones vary sharply by exact address
Some lifestyle markets can exceed military-safe affordability quickly
Air Base and Weapons Station assignments can require different housing strategies
family_readiness.pcsu_family_bluf: Joint Base Charleston can be an excellent family PCS location when the household chooses the right corridor, verifies flood and school risk, and avoids stretching into lifestyle-premium pricing.

arrival_checklist: Confirm whether the assignment is tied primarily to the Air Base side or Weapons Station side.
Verify current gate hours and visitor access procedures through official Joint Base Charleston sources.
Confirm whether on-base housing or off-base housing better fits rank, dependents, BAH, pets, school needs, duty location, and expected assignment length.
Compare Goose Creek, Hanahan, North Charleston, Summerville, Ladson, Moncks Corner, and West Ashley using commute, flood risk, schools, safety, and all-in monthly cost.
Check flood zone, wind/hurricane insurance, HOA, and utility estimates before finalizing a purchase budget.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, commute risk, and assignment-length exposure before committing.
pcs_watchouts: Do not choose a house before confirming Air Base versus Weapons Station duty location.
Do not ignore flood zones or insurance quotes.
Do not assume downtown Charleston or beach-adjacent areas fit military-safe budgets.
Do not rely only on mileage; commute route and gate matter.
School quality and neighborhood fit vary enough to require exact-address verification.
Short assignments may favor renting if transaction costs, insurance, and resale risk are high.

realtor_intelligence.best_client_positioning: Lead with duty-location logic, BAH fit, flood/insurance clarity, school-zone confidence, and commute strategy. Charleston military clients need a guide who understands the difference between pretty and practical.
strong_listing_angles: Commute logic to Joint Base Charleston
Air Base or Weapons Station route clarity
buyer_consultation_angles: Rent versus buy based on assignment length
Air Base versus Weapons Station commute map
All-in payment including taxes, insurance, HOA, flood insurance, and utilities
Goose Creek versus Hanahan versus Summerville versus North Charleston tradeoffs
Future resale appeal to military and civilian buyers
realtor_intelligence.pcsu_realtor_bluf: A strong Charleston realtor should sound like a coastal-risk advisor, commute strategist, and VA-buyer financial filter — not just a lifestyle salesperson.

notes: Market and housing values are PCSUnited planning estimates and should be refreshed periodically.
Gate, visitor, service, medical, and housing office details should be verified against official installation sources before publication or use in routing.
Placeholder map image is used until a dedicated Joint Base Charleston orientation map is created.
Charleston metro pricing varies dramatically between North Charleston, Goose Creek, Summerville, Mount Pleasant, downtown, and island markets.

source_notes: Use official Joint Base Charleston sources for gate hours, visitor control, newcomer guidance, and installation access.
Use official Air Force Housing and local housing office sources for Air Base and Weapons Station housing details.
Use DeCA, AAFES, and TRICARE sources for commissary, exchange, and medical details.
Use Zillow-style indexes, Realtor.com-style market data, local MLS, rental aggregators, HUD, and Census-style demographic sources for housing and market refreshes.
base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited base profiles are planning tools only. They are not official DoD, Air Force, Navy, installation, legal, financial, real estate, lending, insurance, flood-risk, or navigation guidance. Users should verify gate access, housing rules, school zones, medical services, visitor procedures, market prices, flood zones, insurance estimates, commute conditions, and property condition through official and professional sources before making decisions.

rent.provider: HUD / rental listing aggregators / Charleston-area planning estimates

base_profile.provider: PCSUnited planning profile using public-facing installation, housing, and market research structure

parser_notes: Built in the PCSUnited city/base JSON pattern used by Lackland, Andrews, Barksdale, Beale, and Cannon.
All major PCSUnited fields included and adapted for Joint Base Charleston.
Compatible with PCS Snapshot, Base Demographics Shell, Base SaaS, and TOR parsing.
Preserves duplicate value aliases for avg_home_value, average_home_value, avgHome, and city_avg_home.
Preserves duplicate median price aliases for median_list_price, median_sold_price, and median_sale_price.
Preserves by_bedroom entries for 2, 3, 4, and 5-bedroom Market Insight compatibility.

Expanded base_profile to include map image, official links, visitor control center, gates, major services, on-base housing, recommended neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market reality, family readiness, arrival checklist, PCS watchouts, realtor intelligence, data confidence, source notes, and disclaimer.
